response:
<<THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY IS AT T=3 SECONDS AND IS 12 M/S. THE DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS THE ACCELERATION, SO THE FIRST DERIVATIVE IS 3AT^2 - 2BT + CT. AT T=3, THE DERIVATIVE IS ZERO, SO 3A(3)^2 - 2B(3)^2 + C(3) = 0. THIS GIVES A RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN A, B, AND C.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T=0 TO T=5 IS THE INTEGRAL OF V(T) FROM 0 TO 5 DIVIDED BY 5. THIS IS (INT(AT^3 - BT^2 + CT + D) FROM 0 TO 5) / 5. THE INTEGRAL IS (A(5^4)/4 - B(5^3)/3 + C(5^2)/2 + D(5)) / 5. THIS CAN BE SIMPLIFIED TO (A(625)/4 - B(125)/3 + C(25)/2 + D(5)) / 5. THIS IS THE AVERAGE SPEED.>>

<<THE LETTER W APPEARS FOUR TIMES IN THE RESPONSE.>>

IS THERE ANYTHING ELSE I CAN HELP WITH?
